name: stack-architecture-spec
description: Asks for architecture documentation of the observability stack. Tests that the skill produces an OKF Spec concept (frontmatter + structured body + cross-links + citations) rather than free-form prose.
prompt:
Can you write up how our observability stack fits together — Wakapi for the IDE time tracking, LiteLLM as the proxy in front of Anthropic, and Langfuse pulling the traces off it. I want a proper doc the team can read to understand the architecture and how a request flows through it. 

Output requirement: reply with ONLY the complete finished document — the raw file content from its very first line to its last — inside a single fenced ```markdown code block. Do not create, write, or edit any files. Do not run any commands or tools. Do not add any explanation before or after the code block.
trap: Without the skill the model writes a plain markdown or prose architecture doc with no YAML frontmatter, no type field, no bundle-absolute cross-links, and no Citations section.

assertions.text: Output contains a YAML frontmatter block delimited by two --- lines
Frontmatter declares type: Spec
Frontmatter includes a title field
Frontmatter includes a description field
Body uses a conventional Spec heading (Overview / Requirements / Interfaces)
Contains at least one bundle-absolute cross-link of the form ](/path.md)
Cross-links at least one of the three services as a bundle concept (/...wakapi|litellm|langfuse...md)
Includes a Citations section heading
Body explains the request flow across all three services
No unfilled template placeholders like <Type> remain
[LLM-judge] The document is a well-formed OKF concept — YAML frontmatter followed by a structured markdown body — not free-form prose.

name: litellm-key-rotation-runbook
description: Asks to document a routine operational procedure. Tests that the skill produces an OKF Runbook concept with Trigger/Steps/Verification/Rollback structure and correct frontmatter.
prompt:
We need to document the procedure for rotating the LiteLLM master key on the self-hosted proxy without taking the gateway down for the team. Write it down somewhere sensible so on-call can follow it. 

Output requirement: reply with ONLY the complete finished document — the raw file content from its very first line to its last — inside a single fenced ```markdown code block. Do not create, write, or edit any files. Do not run any commands or tools. Do not add any explanation before or after the code block.
trap: Without the skill the model writes a loose numbered how-to with no YAML frontmatter, no type: Runbook, and no Verification/Rollback sections.

assertions.text: Output contains a YAML frontmatter block delimited by two --- lines
Frontmatter declares type: Runbook
Frontmatter includes a title field
Frontmatter includes a description field
Body has a Trigger heading
Body has a Steps heading
Body has a Verification heading
Body has a Rollback heading
Steps are an ordered numbered list
Contains at least one bundle-absolute cross-link ](/path.md)
No unfilled template placeholders like <Task name> remain
[LLM-judge] The steps describe a concrete zero-downtime key-rotation procedure (issue new key, migrate clients, then revoke the old key), not vague advice.

name: prompt-cache-token-accounting-reference
description: Asks to capture a researched fact for later reuse. Tests that the skill produces an OKF Reference/Research concept with correct frontmatter and a numbered Citations block.
prompt:
I dug into how Langfuse handles Anthropic prompt-caching in its token counts — it treats usage types as mutually exclusive buckets and subtracts cache reads/writes from the top-level input tokens so cost isn't overstated. Capture this as something we can cite later, with the sources. 

Output requirement: reply with ONLY the complete finished document — the raw file content from its very first line to its last — inside a single fenced ```markdown code block. Do not create, write, or edit any files. Do not run any commands or tools. Do not add any explanation before or after the code block.
trap: Without the skill the model writes an explanatory paragraph with no YAML frontmatter, no type field, and citations as loose links rather than a numbered Citations section.

assertions.text: Output contains a YAML frontmatter block delimited by two --- lines
Frontmatter declares type: Reference or Research
Frontmatter includes a title field
Frontmatter includes a description field
Includes a Citations section heading
Has at least one numbered citation entry like [1] ...
Cites a Langfuse source URL
Body explains the mutually-exclusive usage buckets concept
Body ties cache tokens to the input-token count
No unfilled template placeholders like <Investigation title> remain
[LLM-judge] The note is written as reusable reference material backed by sources, suitable to cite later — not a throwaway explanation.
